campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council met to accept a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help fund youth scholarships for recreation programs. The council also discussed extending a temporary rule and moving forward with plans to build a bike and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including submitting grant applications to help pay for it.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen for youth recreation scholarships
Extension of interim ordinance #2326 (temporary rule)
Hamilton Avenue bicycle/pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 - grant application and feasibility study approval

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council met to decide how to distribute grant money to different community organizations and events. They approved funding for specific groups that applied for grants, decided how much money the competitive event grant program would receive, and made some rule changes about how community events can get funding and close streets.
keyTopics: Approved which organizations will receive grant funding and how much money each gets
Set the total budget for the Competitive Event Grant Program for the year
Updated rules for Community Event and Street Closure Grants to clarify how they work

san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting on March 25, 2026 provided information about how residents can watch the proceedings. The meeting offered live translations in over 50 languages and multiple ways to tune in, though this particular agenda focused on access and viewing options rather than specific policy decisions.
keyTopics: Live translation services available in 50+ languages
Multiple viewing options: Cable Channel 26, city website, YouTube, and Zoom
Phone dial-in access for residents without internet
Meeting broadcast information for public observation

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 25, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council's zoning hearing that was supposed to happen on March 25, 2026 has been cancelled. The hearing will now take place on April 8, 2026 instead. If you had plans to attend or submit comments about zoning issues, you'll need to wait until the new date in April.
keyTopics: March 25 zoning hearing cancelled
Rescheduled to April 8, 2026
Administrative zoning matters affected

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review funding applications for community development grants that help improve housing and support services in Sunnyvale. The meeting included approval of previous meeting notes and presentations from organizations seeking federal money to support local housing and human services programs.
keyTopics: Community Development Block Grant funding applications reviewed
Federal housing and social services funding decisions
February meeting minutes approved
Organizations pitching for local development funding

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council focused on helping local businesses grow and thrive. They discussed ways to reach out to shop owners about a license amnesty program, promote local shopping through summer events, and offer financial incentives to attract new retail stores and startups to the city.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping business owners catch up on compliance
Outreach to shopping centers and malls to increase participation in city programs
Summer of Celebrations campaign to encourage residents to shop locally
Public-private partnerships to sponsor city events
Economic incentives to attract new retail and startup businesses

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 24, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting where they addressed several delayed agenda items, approved a plan to remove weeds from properties around the city as part of a county-wide program, and opened discussion on how federal housing grant money will be spent next year. Residents had the chance to weigh in on how approximately $5-10 million in HUD housing funds should be used to support local housing needs.
keyTopics: Weed removal program for overgrown properties
Federal HUD housing grant spending for 2026-27
Multiple agenda items postponed or reconsidered
Public comment period on housing fund allocation

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026, to recognize Women's History Month and approve two agreements. The council approved a disaster relief partnership with the American Red Cross to use city facilities during emergencies, and authorized a three-year contract with Insight Public Sector for email security software (Mimecast) costing about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
American Red Cross disaster facility agreement
Mimecast email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late packet report added to agenda
